docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Smeli

Smeli is the Scholarly MEtadata Lookup Interface: a DOI-aware metadata lookup library and command-line tool for scholarly papers.

Smeli can search and normalize metadata from OpenAlex, Crossref, DataCite, arXiv, and doi.org BibTeX content negotiation. DOI is important, but it is not required: DOI-less arXiv and OpenAlex records are valid Smeli candidates.

docs.title: Two ways to use Smeli
docs.text:
Use the command-line program when you want an interactive lookup tool:

smeli
smeli 10.1126/science.1102081
smeli still building the memex davies
smeli 0000-0002-0254-6627

Use the Python API when another program wants candidate records, identifier normalization, metadata lookups, or BibTeX generation:

from smeli import get_paper_candidates, candidate_to_bibtex

candidates = get_paper_candidates(query="still building the memex davies")
for candidate in candidates:
    print(candidate["title"], candidate.get("doi"))

if candidates:
    print(candidate_to_bibtex(candidates[0]))

docs.title: Start here
docs.text:
• API Overview explains the public API modules and recommended entrypoints.
  • Candidate Records documents the dictionary shape used throughout Smeli.
  • Examples shows common library calls.
  • Reference contains generated API reference pages from the public docstrings.

docs.title: Some sample DOIs
docs.text:
• Attention is All you Need: 10.65215/2q58a426 (DataCite)
  • BERT: 10.48550/arXiv.1810.04805 (DataCite)
  • Still Building the Memex: 10.1145/1897816.1897840 (Crossref)
  • XGBoost: 10.1145/2939672.2939785 (Crossref)

docs.title: Some sample ORCIDs
docs.text:
These are useful for trying the author-oriented lookup path:

  • Geoffrey Hinton: 0000-0002-8063-7209
  • Jennifer Doudna: 0000-0001-9161-999X
  • Katalin Karikó: 0000-0002-1864-3851
  • Tim Berners-Lee: 0000-0003-1279-3709

docs.title: Questions and feedback
docs.text:
For bug reports, feature requests, or API questions, please open an issue on the Smeli GitHub repository.

For other questions, contact Stephen Davies.

docs.title: API Overview
docs.text:
Smeli's public API is defined by each module's __all__ list. Names not included in __all__ are private implementation details and may change without notice.

The top-level package re-exports the public library API, so this is the normal import style:

from smeli import get_paper_candidates, search_orcids, candidate_to_bibtex

You can also import from specific modules when that makes the code clearer:

from smeli.sources import get_candidate_from_doi
from smeli.normalize import extract_orcid

docs.title: Public modules
docs.text: Module Purpose smeli.normalize Pure identifier normalization helpers for DOI, ORCID, and arXiv IDs. smeli.candidates Pure local helpers for canonicalizing, scoring, comparing, and merging candidate records. smeli.sources Networked metadata lookups against Crossref, DataCite, OpenAlex, arXiv, and doi.org. smeli.bibtex Small BibTeX parser/printer plus Smeli fallback BibTeX generation. smeli.cli The main() console-script entrypoint for the smeli command.

docs.title: Recommended entrypoints
docs.text:
Most package users should start with get_paper_candidates():

from smeli import get_paper_candidates

candidates = get_paper_candidates(query="opinion dynamics starnini")

Use get_paper_candidates_from_identifier() when the user supplied a DOI, arXiv ID, OpenAlex Work ID, or ORCID:

from smeli import get_paper_candidates_from_identifier

papers = get_paper_candidates_from_identifier("0000-0002-0254-6627")

Use get_candidate_from_doi(), get_candidate_from_arxiv_id(), or get_candidate_from_openalex_id() when your code already knows it is resolving a specific paper identifier. Use search_orcids() when you have an author name fragment and want possible author/ORCID matches.

docs.title: Return conventions
docs.text:
Smeli uses simple Python data structures:

  • candidate lookups return list[dict[str, Any]]
  • single-paper lookups return dict[str, Any] | None
  • metadata lookups return dict[str, Any] | None
  • get_orcids() returns list[str]
  • source-specific ORCID helpers and search_orcids() return list[dict[str, Any]]
  • BibTeX lookup returns str | None

Expected misses return None or []. Real network or parsing problems may also print short diagnostic messages.

docs.title: Stability rule
docs.text: Only names in __all__ are public. Leading-underscore names are private, even if tests import them internally.

docs.title: Candidate Records
docs.text:
A candidate is Smeli's normal dictionary representation for a scholarly paper or paper-like output. A candidate may describe a journal article, conference paper, arXiv preprint, dataset, software record, repository item, or another scholarly output.

Smeli deliberately uses dictionaries rather than a custom class because source APIs vary and the command-line tool benefits from flexible partial records.

docs.title: Common keys
docs.text: Key Type Meaning title str Paper title, when known. authors list[str] Author display names. year int | str | None Publication year, when known. venue str Journal, conference, arXiv, or container/source name. publisher str Publisher or host organization. type str Source-specific publication/resource type. doi str | None Normalized bare DOI, or None. arxiv_id str | None Bare arXiv ID without version suffix where possible. openalex_id str OpenAlex Work URL/ID when available. url str Best landing URL known to Smeli. cited_by_count int Best available citation count after merging source records. citation_source str Source that supplied the selected citation count, when known. citation_sources dict[str, int] Per-source citation counts retained during merging. metadata_sources list[str] Sources that contributed metadata to the merged candidate. score int Smeli's local relevance score for display/ranking. citation_score int Weak citation-count tie-breaker used during ranking. match_note str Optional explanation of a fallback match path.

docs.title: Canonicalization
docs.text:
Call canonical_candidate() when you have a partial record and want Smeli's defaults and identifier normalization:

from smeli import canonical_candidate

candidate = canonical_candidate({
    "title": "Example Paper",
    "doi": "https://doi.org/10.1234/example",
    "source": "ExampleSource",
})

Canonicalization copies the input dictionary, normalizes DOI/arXiv identifiers, fills common optional fields, creates or updates metadata_sources, and derives a landing URL when possible.

docs.title: Merging
docs.text:
Source APIs often describe the same paper slightly differently. Use merge_candidate_list() to collapse likely duplicates across source results:

from smeli import merge_candidate_list

merged = merge_candidate_list(crossref_candidates + openalex_candidates)

Duplicate detection prefers shared identifiers: DOI, arXiv ID, or OpenAlex ID. When identifiers are missing, Smeli falls back to strong title/year/author similarity.

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
The command-line program is installed through the project script entrypoint:

[project.scripts]
smeli = "smeli.cli:main"

Run the interactive menu with:

smeli

Show command-line usage with:

smeli -h
# or
smeli --help

Run a one-shot lookup with a DOI, ORCID, arXiv/OpenAlex identifier, or free-form bibliographic query:

smeli 10.1126/science.1102081
smeli 0000-0002-0254-6627
smeli still building the memex davies

You can also provide fielded search terms directly from the shell:

smeli --author "Stephen Davies" --title "Still building the memex" --year 1995
smeli -a "Starnini" -t "opinion dynamics"
smeli --identifier 10.1126/science.1102081

Use --list with either style to force a selectable result list even when Smeli finds a single match:

smeli --list --author "Stephen Davies" --title "Still building the memex"

smeli.cli.main() is public because it is the console-script target. Python package users should usually call functions from smeli.sources instead.

docs.title: Test suite
docs.text:
Smeli's tests are intentionally fast and offline. They use fake or monkeypatched API responses rather than live requests to doi.org, OpenAlex, Crossref, DataCite, or arXiv.

pytest

docs.title: Compile check
docs.text: python3 -m py_compile smeli/*.py

docs.title: Documentation dependencies
docs.text:
Install the documentation extras to build or serve the site locally:

pip install -e '.[docs]'

Then run:

mkdocs serve

or build static HTML:

mkdocs build

The static site is written to site/ and can be hosted with GitHub Pages or any static web host.

docs.title: Public API rule
docs.text: The public API is defined by __all__. Every public name should have a useful docstring and a reference page entry. Leading-underscore names are private implementation details.

docs.title: Normalize user-supplied identifiers
docs.text:
from smeli import clean_doi, extract_arxiv_id, extract_orcid

print(clean_doi("https://doi.org/10.1126/science.1102081"))
print(extract_arxiv_id("https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.01234v2"))
print(extract_orcid("https://orcid.org/0000-0002-0254-6627"))

docs.title: Search by free-form bibliographic query
docs.text:
from smeli import get_paper_candidates

candidates = get_paper_candidates(query="still building the memex davies")

for candidate in candidates[:5]:
    print(candidate["score"], candidate["title"], candidate.get("doi"))

docs.title: Search by fielded metadata
docs.text:
from smeli import get_paper_candidates

candidates = get_paper_candidates(
    title="Still building the memex",
    author="Davies",
    year=2011,
)

docs.title: Resolve a paper identifier
docs.text:
from smeli import get_paper_candidates_from_identifier

papers = get_paper_candidates_from_identifier("10.1126/science.1102081")

docs.title: Resolve an ORCID
docs.text:
ORCID identifies a person, not a paper, so Smeli returns a list of papers:

from smeli import get_paper_candidates_from_identifier

papers = get_paper_candidates_from_identifier("0000-0002-0254-6627")
for paper in papers:
    print(paper.get("year"), paper.get("title"))

docs.title: Generate fallback BibTeX
docs.text:
from smeli import candidate_to_bibtex, get_paper_candidates

candidates = get_paper_candidates(query="still building the memex davies")
if candidates:
    print(candidate_to_bibtex(candidates[0]))

When a DOI exists, prefer doi.org BibTeX first:

from smeli import candidate_to_bibtex, get_bibtex_from_doi

bibtex = get_bibtex_from_doi("10.1126/science.1102081")
if bibtex is None:
    bibtex = candidate_to_bibtex(candidate)

docs.title: smeli.bibtex
docs.text:
BibTeX parsing, pretty-printing, and Smeli fallback generation.

BibTeX parsing, pretty-printing, and generation.

Smeli prefers doi.org-provided BibTeX when it is available for a DOI. These helpers support parsing and displaying that BibTeX, and generating a conservative fallback BibTeX-like entry from Smeli candidate metadata.

docs.title: candidate_to_bibtex
docs.text:
candidate_to_bibtex(candidate: dict[str, Any]) -> str

Generate a conservative BibTeX-like entry from candidate metadata.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default candidate dict[str, Any]

A Smeli candidate dictionary.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description str str

A BibTeX-like string using available fields such as title, authors, year, venue, DOI, arXiv ID, and URL.

Notes

This is a fallback formatter, not a replacement for publisher- or resolver-provided BibTeX. Prefer smeli.sources.get_bibtex_from_doi() when a DOI is available and doi.org content negotiation succeeds.

docs.title: print_bibtex
docs.text:
print_bibtex(bibtex: str) -> None

Print a BibTeX entry in a friendlier field-by-field format.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default bibtex str

Text containing one BibTeX entry.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description None None

Output is written to standard output.

Notes

The raw BibTeX is printed after the field-by-field display so it can still be copied into BibTeX-aware tools.

docs.title: parse_bibtex_entry
docs.text:
parse_bibtex_entry(bibtex: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None

Parse a single BibTeX entry into a simple dictionary.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default bibtex str

Text containing one BibTeX entry.

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any] | None

dict[str, Any] | None: A dictionary with entry_type, cite_key, and fields keys, or None if the text does not look like a normal single BibTeX entry.

Notes

This parser is intentionally small. It handles ordinary BibTeX returned by DOI content negotiation, but it is not a full BibTeX parser.

docs.title: make_cite_key
docs.text:
make_cite_key(candidate: dict[str, Any]) -> str

Create a compact citation key from first-author surname and year.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default candidate dict[str, Any]

A Smeli candidate dictionary.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description str str

A lower-case key in Kurrent/Smeli style, such as "davies2011" or "starnini2025". Missing authors fall back to "paper" and missing years fall back to "nd".

docs.title: smeli.candidates
docs.text:
Local helpers for canonicalizing, scoring, comparing, and merging Smeli candidate records.

Candidate conversion, scoring, and merging helpers.

A candidate is Smeli's normal paper-record dictionary. It may represent a journal article, conference paper, arXiv preprint, dataset, software record, or another scholarly output. Common keys include title, authors, year, venue, publisher, doi, arxiv_id, openalex_id, url, cited_by_count, metadata_sources, score, and citation_score.

The public functions in this module are pure local helpers: they do not perform network requests.

docs.title: canonical_candidate
docs.text:
canonical_candidate(candidate: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]

Return a normalized copy of a candidate dictionary.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default candidate dict[str, Any]

A partial Smeli candidate record. Missing optional fields are allowed.

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

dict[str, Any]: A new dictionary with normalized DOI/arXiv identifiers, default values for common optional fields, a metadata_sources list, and a best-effort landing url when an identifier is available.

Notes

The input dictionary is copied before modification.

docs.title: candidate_score
docs.text:
candidate_score(candidate: dict[str, Any], *, author: str | None = None, title: str | None = None, year: str | int | None = None) -> tuple[int, int]

Return a local relevance score for a paper candidate.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default candidate dict[str, Any]

A Smeli candidate dictionary.

required author str | None

Optional author name or fragment supplied by the user.

None title str | None

Optional title or title fragment supplied by the user.

None year str | int | None

Optional publication year supplied by the user.

None

Returns:

Type Description tuple[int, int]

tuple[int, int]: A (score, citation_bonus) tuple. score reflects local bibliographic relevance; citation_bonus is a weak tie-breaker derived from citation count.

Notes

The score is intentionally heuristic. It is useful for ranking plausible candidates, not for measuring scholarly relevance in an academic sense.

docs.title: candidates_are_same
docs.text:
candidates_are_same(a: dict[str, Any], b: dict[str, Any]) -> bool

Return whether two candidates appear to describe the same paper.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default a dict[str, Any]

First Smeli candidate dictionary.

required b dict[str, Any]

Second Smeli candidate dictionary.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description bool bool

True if the candidates share a DOI, arXiv ID, OpenAlex ID, or have a strong title/year/author match; otherwise False.

docs.title: merge_candidates
docs.text:
merge_candidates(primary: dict[str, Any], secondary: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]

Merge two candidate dictionaries describing the same paper.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default primary dict[str, Any]

The preferred candidate record. Existing values are generally kept.

required secondary dict[str, Any]

A second candidate record used to fill gaps and add sources.

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

dict[str, Any]: A canonicalized candidate dictionary containing the best available fields from both inputs.

Notes

This function does not first verify that the records match. Call candidates_are_same() before merging untrusted pairs.

docs.title: merge_candidate_list
docs.text:
merge_candidate_list(candidates: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]

Merge a list of candidate dictionaries across metadata sources.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default candidates list[dict[str, Any]]

Candidate records from one or more source APIs.

required

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

list[dict[str, Any]]: A list of canonicalized candidate records with likely duplicates collapsed using candidates_are_same() and merge_candidates().

docs.title: bibliographic_query
docs.text:
bibliographic_query(author: str | None = None, title: str | None = None, year: str | int | None = None) -> str

Build a compact bibliographic query string from available fields.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default author str | None

Optional author name or fragment.

None title str | None

Optional title or title fragment.

None year str | int | None

Optional publication year.

None

Returns:

Name Type Description str str

A single space-separated query string containing non-empty fields in title-author-year order.

docs.title: smeli.cli
docs.text:
Console-script entrypoint for the smeli command.

Command-line interface for Smeli.

The library-facing public API of this module is intentionally tiny: main() is the console-script entrypoint used by the smeli command. Display helpers, prompts, color constants, and one-shot argument handling are private implementation details.

docs.title: main
docs.text:
main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> None

Run the scholarly metadata lookup CLI.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default argv list[str] | None

Optional command-line arguments excluding the program name. When None, arguments are read from sys.argv.

None

Returns:

Name Type Description None None

Results are printed to standard output and interactive prompts read from standard input.

Notes

Normal package users should prefer the lookup functions in :mod:smeli.sources. This function is public primarily because it is the console-script target for the smeli command.

docs.title: smeli.normalize
docs.text:
Pure helpers for DOI, ORCID, and arXiv identifier normalization.

Normalization and identifier helpers for Smeli.

This module contains small, side-effect-free helpers for recognizing and normalizing scholarly identifiers. Public functions accept messy user input where practical: bare identifiers, resolver URLs, common prefixes, and leading or trailing whitespace.

docs.title: clean_doi
docs.text:
clean_doi(value: str | None) -> str | None

Normalize common DOI representations to a bare DOI string.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default value str | None

A DOI-like string, DOI URL, doi:-prefixed value, or None.

required

Returns:

Type Description str | None

str | None: The normalized bare DOI, preserving DOI case where present, or None if the input is empty or does not contain a valid-looking DOI.

Examples:

clean_doi("https://doi.org/10.1126/science.1102081") returns "10.1126/science.1102081".

docs.title: looks_like_doi
docs.text:
looks_like_doi(value: str | None) -> bool

Return whether text contains a valid-looking DOI.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default value str | None

Text that may contain a bare DOI or DOI resolver URL.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description bool bool

True when clean_doi() can extract a DOI; otherwise False.

docs.title: extract_orcid
docs.text:
extract_orcid(value: Any | None) -> str | None

Extract and normalize a bare ORCID iD from text, URL, or record.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default value Any | None

Text that may contain a bare ORCID iD or an orcid.org URL, or a nested candidate/source record that may contain an ORCID value.

required

Returns:

Type Description str | None

str | None: The normalized bare ORCID iD, such as "0000-0002-0254-6627", or None when no ORCID-like identifier is present.

docs.title: looks_like_orcid
docs.text:
looks_like_orcid(value: str | None) -> bool

Return whether text contains an ORCID iD or ORCID URL.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default value str | None

Text that may contain an ORCID identifier.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description bool bool

True when extract_orcid() can extract an ORCID iD; otherwise False.

docs.title: orcid_url
docs.text:
orcid_url(orcid: str | None) -> str | None

Return the canonical ORCID URL for an ORCID-like value.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default orcid str | None

A bare ORCID iD, ORCID URL, or other ORCID-like text.

required

Returns:

Type Description str | None

str | None: A canonical URL such as "https://orcid.org/0000-0002-0254-6627", or None if the input does not contain an ORCID iD.

docs.title: extract_arxiv_id
docs.text:
extract_arxiv_id(value: str | None) -> str | None

Extract a modern or old-style arXiv ID from text or URL.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default value str | None

Text that may contain a bare arXiv ID, arXiv:-prefixed ID, or an arXiv abstract/PDF URL.

required

Returns:

Type Description str | None

str | None: The arXiv ID as written, including a version suffix when present, or None when no arXiv identifier is present.

docs.title: base_arxiv_id
docs.text:
base_arxiv_id(arxiv_id: str | None) -> str | None

Strip an arXiv version suffix for deduplication.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default arxiv_id str | None

An arXiv identifier such as "2501.01234v2".

required

Returns:

Type Description str | None

str | None: The identifier without its trailing version suffix, such as "2501.01234", or None for empty input.

docs.title: make_arxiv_url

Return the canonical arXiv abstract URL for an ID.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default arxiv_id str | None

A bare arXiv identifier, optionally with an arXiv: prefix, URL wrapper, or version suffix.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description str str

The canonical abstract URL, or the empty string if the input is empty.

docs.title: canonical_candidate
docs.text:
canonical_candidate(candidate: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]

Return a normalized copy of a candidate dictionary.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default candidate dict[str, Any]

A partial Smeli candidate record. Missing optional fields are allowed.

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

dict[str, Any]: A new dictionary with normalized DOI/arXiv identifiers, default values for common optional fields, a metadata_sources list, and a best-effort landing url when an identifier is available.

Notes

The input dictionary is copied before modification.

docs.title: candidate_score
docs.text:

Return a local relevance score for a paper candidate.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default candidate dict[str, Any]

A Smeli candidate dictionary.

required author str | None

Optional author name or fragment supplied by the user.

None title str | None

Optional title or title fragment supplied by the user.

None year str | int | None

Optional publication year supplied by the user.

None

Returns:

Type Description tuple[int, int]

tuple[int, int]: A (score, citation_bonus) tuple. score reflects local bibliographic relevance; citation_bonus is a weak tie-breaker derived from citation count.

Notes

The score is intentionally heuristic. It is useful for ranking plausible candidates, not for measuring scholarly relevance in an academic sense.

docs.text:

Return whether two candidates appear to describe the same paper.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default a dict[str, Any]

First Smeli candidate dictionary.

required b dict[str, Any]

Second Smeli candidate dictionary.

required

Returns:

True if the candidates share a DOI, arXiv ID, OpenAlex ID, or have a strong title/year/author match; otherwise False.

docs.title: merge_candidates
docs.text:
merge_candidates(primary: dict[str, Any], secondary: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]

Merge two candidate dictionaries describing the same paper.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default primary dict[str, Any]

The preferred candidate record. Existing values are generally kept.

required secondary dict[str, Any]

A second candidate record used to fill gaps and add sources.

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

dict[str, Any]: A canonicalized candidate dictionary containing the best available fields from both inputs.

Notes

This function does not first verify that the records match. Call candidates_are_same() before merging untrusted pairs.

docs.title: merge_candidate_list
docs.text:
merge_candidate_list(candidates: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]

Merge a list of candidate dictionaries across metadata sources.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default candidates list[dict[str, Any]]

Candidate records from one or more source APIs.

required

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

list[dict[str, Any]]: A list of canonicalized candidate records with likely duplicates collapsed using candidates_are_same() and merge_candidates().

docs.title: bibliographic_query
docs.text:
bibliographic_query(author: str | None = None, title: str | None = None, year: str | int | None = None) -> str

Build a compact bibliographic query string from available fields.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default author str | None

Optional author name or fragment.

None title str | None

Optional title or title fragment.

None year str | int | None

Optional publication year.

None

Returns:

Name Type Description str str

A single space-separated query string containing non-empty fields in title-author-year order.

docs.title: get_paper_candidates
docs.text:
get_paper_candidates(author: str | None = None, title: str | None = None, year: str | int | None = None, identifier: str | None = None, query: str | None = None, *, progress: Callable[[str], None] | None = None) -> list[dict[str, Any]]

Search all configured metadata sources and return merged candidates.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default author str | None

Optional author name or fragment for fielded search.

None title str | None

Optional title or title fragment for fielded search.

None year str | int | None

Optional publication year for filtering/scoring.

None identifier str | None

Optional DOI, arXiv ID/URL, ORCID, or OpenAlex Work ID/URL. When supplied, identifier resolution takes precedence over fielded search.

None query str | None

Optional free-form bibliographic query. Used when no identifier is supplied.

None progress Callable[[str], None] | None

Optional callback for source-level progress messages. The CLI passes print here; ordinary Python API calls are quiet by default.

None

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

list[dict[str, Any]]: A score-sorted list of merged Smeli candidate dictionaries. Duplicate paper records from multiple sources are collapsed and annotated with metadata_sources.

Notes

This is the highest-level public lookup API. It does not print during ordinary Python API use unless a progress callback is supplied.

docs.title: get_metadata
docs.text:
get_metadata(identifier: str | None) -> dict[str, Any] | None

Fetch structured metadata for a DOI, arXiv ID, or OpenAlex Work ID.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default identifier str | None

A DOI, DOI URL, arXiv ID/URL, or OpenAlex Work ID/URL.

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any] | None

dict[str, Any] | None: A source-agnostic metadata dictionary when Smeli finds usable metadata, or None when no configured source has a usable record.

Notes

DOI lookup tries Crossref first, then DataCite, then OpenAlex. arXiv and OpenAlex identifiers are resolved through their native APIs. Source- specific helpers remain available when callers deliberately want a particular service.

docs.title: get_orcids
docs.text:
get_orcids(value: Any | None) -> list[str]

Return normalized ORCID iDs from an identifier or metadata record.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default value Any | None

A DOI, ORCID iD/URL, or a nested metadata/candidate record that may contain ORCID values.

required

Returns:

Type Description list[str]

list[str]: Normalized bare ORCID iDs, deduplicated in discovery order.

Notes

DOI lookup checks OpenAlex, Crossref, and DataCite. Source-specific helpers return richer {"name": ..., "orcid": ...} records; this high-level helper returns just the normalized identifiers.

docs.title: search_orcids
docs.text:
search_orcids(name: str | None, affiliation: str | None = None, max_results: int = 10) -> list[dict[str, Any]]

Search for author ORCID iDs by partial author name.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default name str | None

Author name or name fragment to search for.

required affiliation str | None

Optional institution or affiliation fragment used for local filtering of returned author candidates.

None max_results int

Maximum number of ORCID-bearing candidate records to return.

10

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

list[dict[str, Any]]: Candidate author records with keys such as name, orcid, orcid_url, openalex_id, affiliation, affiliations, works_count, cited_by_count, source, and sometimes relevance.

Notes

This searches OpenAlex author records. It returns candidate people, not papers, and deliberately excludes author records without ORCID iDs.

docs.title: get_paper_candidates_from_identifier

Resolve a DOI, arXiv ID/URL, ORCID, or OpenAlex Work ID/URL.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default identifier str

A scholarly identifier or resolver URL.

required

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

list[dict[str, Any]]: A list of Smeli candidate dictionaries. Paper identifiers usually return zero or one candidate. ORCID identifiers may return many candidates because they identify an author/person rather than a paper.

docs.title: get_candidate_from_doi

A bare DOI, DOI URL, or DOI-like string.

required

dict[str, Any] | None: A merged Smeli candidate dictionary enriched from Crossref, DataCite, and OpenAlex where available. If no external metadata is found for a valid DOI, returns a minimal DOI-only candidate.

required

Name Type Description Default openalex_id str

An OpenAlex Work ID such as "W2741809807" or a full openalex.org Work URL.

required

docs.title: get_paper_candidates_from_orcid
docs.text:
get_paper_candidates_from_orcid(orcid: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]

Fetch paper candidates for an author ORCID using OpenAlex.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default orcid str

A bare ORCID iD or ORCID URL.

required

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

list[dict[str, Any]]: A list of Smeli candidate dictionaries, sorted by OpenAlex publication date descending before later Smeli ranking/merging.

Notes

ORCID identifies a person, not a paper, so this function returns a list of papers. OpenAlex may return no papers for a valid ORCID when its author links lack that ORCID.

required

A bare DOI, DOI URL, or DOI-like string.

required

dict[str, Any] | None: A metadata dictionary with keys such as source, doi, title, authors, journal, publisher, citations, year, type, and url; or None if DataCite has no usable record.

Notes

DataCite is a separate DOI registration agency from Crossref. It is often useful for datasets, software, preprints, and repository items.

Retrieve BibTeX from doi.org content negotiation.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default doi str

A bare DOI, DOI URL, or DOI-like string.

required

str | None: A raw BibTeX string from the DOI resolver, or None when no BibTeX is returned.

Notes

This goes through the DOI resolver rather than Crossref directly, so it may succeed for DOIs outside Crossref's registry.

A bare DOI, DOI URL, or DOI-like string.

required

Returns:

list[dict[str, str]]: A list of dictionaries with name and orcid keys, or an empty list when OpenAlex has no ORCID data for the paper.

A bare DOI, DOI URL, or DOI-like string.

required

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, str]]

list[dict[str, str]]: A list of dictionaries with name and orcid keys, or an empty list when Crossref has no ORCID data for the paper.

Notes

Crossref ORCID coverage depends on what the publisher supplied.

A bare DOI, DOI URL, or DOI-like string.

required

list[dict[str, str]]: A list of dictionaries with name and orcid keys, or an empty list when DataCite has no ORCID data for the paper.

Notes

DataCite ORCID coverage is common for repository and dataset records, but still depends on what the depositing source supplied.

docs.title: get_doi_from_crossref

Return Crossref paper candidates that have DOIs.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default author str | None

Optional author name or fragment.

None title str | None

Optional title or title fragment.

None year str | int | None

Optional publication year.

None

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

list[dict[str, Any]]: Crossref candidate dictionaries filtered to records with a DOI.

Notes

This is a compatibility/convenience helper from Smeli's DOI-focused origins. New code usually wants get_paper_candidates_from_crossref() or get_paper_candidates() instead.

docs.text:

Name Type Description Default author str | None

Optional title or title fragment.

None year str | int | None

Optional publication year.

docs.title: candidate_to_bibtex
docs.text:
candidate_to_bibtex(candidate: dict[str, Any]) -> str

Generate a conservative BibTeX-like entry from candidate metadata.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default candidate dict[str, Any]

A Smeli candidate dictionary.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description str str

A BibTeX-like string using available fields such as title, authors, year, venue, DOI, arXiv ID, and URL.

Notes

This is a fallback formatter, not a replacement for publisher- or resolver-provided BibTeX. Prefer smeli.sources.get_bibtex_from_doi() when a DOI is available and doi.org content negotiation succeeds.

docs.title: print_bibtex
docs.text:
print_bibtex(bibtex: str) -> None

Print a BibTeX entry in a friendlier field-by-field format.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default bibtex str

Text containing one BibTeX entry.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description None None

Output is written to standard output.

Notes

The raw BibTeX is printed after the field-by-field display so it can still be copied into BibTeX-aware tools.

docs.title: parse_bibtex_entry
docs.text:
parse_bibtex_entry(bibtex: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None

Parse a single BibTeX entry into a simple dictionary.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default bibtex str

Text containing one BibTeX entry.

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any] | None

dict[str, Any] | None: A dictionary with entry_type, cite_key, and fields keys, or None if the text does not look like a normal single BibTeX entry.

Notes

This parser is intentionally small. It handles ordinary BibTeX returned by DOI content negotiation, but it is not a full BibTeX parser.

docs.title: make_cite_key
docs.text:
make_cite_key(candidate: dict[str, Any]) -> str

Create a compact citation key from first-author surname and year.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default candidate dict[str, Any]

A Smeli candidate dictionary.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description str str

A lower-case key in Kurrent/Smeli style, such as "davies2011" or "starnini2025". Missing authors fall back to "paper" and missing years fall back to "nd".

docs.title: main
docs.text:
main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> None

Run the scholarly metadata lookup CLI.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default argv list[str] | None

Optional command-line arguments excluding the program name. When None, arguments are read from sys.argv.

None

Returns:

Name Type Description None None

Results are printed to standard output and interactive prompts read from standard input.

Notes

Normal package users should prefer the lookup functions in :mod:smeli.sources. This function is public primarily because it is the console-script target for the smeli command.

docs.title: smeli.sources
docs.text:
Metadata source lookups and cross-source orchestration.

This module is the main networked public API for Smeli. It queries OpenAlex, Crossref, DataCite, arXiv, and doi.org, then returns either structured metadata dictionaries, ORCID lists, BibTeX strings, or Smeli candidate dictionaries.

Functions return None or an empty list when no useful result is found. Expected misses are handled quietly where practical; real network and parsing errors may print short diagnostic messages.

docs.title: get_paper_candidates
docs.text:
get_paper_candidates(author: str | None = None, title: str | None = None, year: str | int | None = None, identifier: str | None = None, query: str | None = None, *, progress: Callable[[str], None] | None = None) -> list[dict[str, Any]]

Search all configured metadata sources and return merged candidates.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default author str | None

Optional author name or fragment for fielded search.

None title str | None

Optional title or title fragment for fielded search.

None year str | int | None

Optional publication year for filtering/scoring.

None identifier str | None

Optional DOI, arXiv ID/URL, ORCID, or OpenAlex Work ID/URL. When supplied, identifier resolution takes precedence over fielded search.

None query str | None

Optional free-form bibliographic query. Used when no identifier is supplied.

None progress Callable[[str], None] | None

Optional callback for source-level progress messages. The CLI passes print here; ordinary Python API calls are quiet by default.

None

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

list[dict[str, Any]]: A score-sorted list of merged Smeli candidate dictionaries. Duplicate paper records from multiple sources are collapsed and annotated with metadata_sources.

Notes

This is the highest-level public lookup API. It does not print during ordinary Python API use unless a progress callback is supplied.

docs.title: get_orcids_from_openalex
docs.text:
get_orcids_from_openalex(doi: str) -> list[dict[str, str]]

Extract author ORCIDs for a DOI using OpenAlex.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default doi str

A bare DOI, DOI URL, or DOI-like string.

required

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, str]]

list[dict[str, str]]: A list of dictionaries with name and orcid keys, or an empty list when OpenAlex has no ORCID data for the paper.

docs.title: get_orcids_from_crossref
docs.text:
get_orcids_from_crossref(doi: str) -> list[dict[str, str]]

Extract author ORCIDs from Crossref metadata.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default doi str

A bare DOI, DOI URL, or DOI-like string.

required

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, str]]

list[dict[str, str]]: A list of dictionaries with name and orcid keys, or an empty list when Crossref has no ORCID data for the paper.

Notes

Crossref ORCID coverage depends on what the publisher supplied.

docs.title: get_orcids_from_datacite
docs.text:
get_orcids_from_datacite(doi: str) -> list[dict[str, str]]

Extract author ORCIDs from DataCite metadata.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default doi str

A bare DOI, DOI URL, or DOI-like string.

required

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, str]]

list[dict[str, str]]: A list of dictionaries with name and orcid keys, or an empty list when DataCite has no ORCID data for the paper.

Notes

DataCite ORCID coverage is common for repository and dataset records, but still depends on what the depositing source supplied.

docs.title: get_doi_from_crossref
docs.text:
get_doi_from_crossref(author: str | None = None, title: str | None = None, year: str | int | None = None) -> list[dict[str, Any]]

Return Crossref paper candidates that have DOIs.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default author str | None

Optional author name or fragment.

None title str | None

Optional title or title fragment.

None year str | int | None

Optional publication year.

None

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

list[dict[str, Any]]: Crossref candidate dictionaries filtered to records with a DOI.

Notes

This is a compatibility/convenience helper from Smeli's DOI-focused origins. New code usually wants get_paper_candidates_from_crossref() or get_paper_candidates() instead.

docs.title: get_doi_from_openalex
docs.text:
get_doi_from_openalex(author: str | None = None, title: str | None = None, year: str | int | None = None) -> list[dict[str, Any]]

Return OpenAlex paper candidates that have DOIs.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default author str | None

Optional author name or fragment.

None title str | None

Optional title or title fragment.

None year str | int | None

Optional publication year.

None

Returns:

Type Description list[dict[str, Any]]

list[dict[str, Any]]: OpenAlex candidate dictionaries filtered to records with a DOI.

Notes

This is a compatibility/convenience helper from Smeli's DOI-focused origins. New code usually wants get_paper_candidates_from_openalex() or get_paper_candidates() instead.
